revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Domain|ja|相馬中村藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:Sōma Shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]]

During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:Sōma Shrines]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Bosatsu|qq=}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma clan|qq=}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Sekigahara|qq=}} in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Domain|ja|相馬中村藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Domain|qq=}}. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boshin War|qq=}}. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:Sōma Shrines]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]]

Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boshin War|qq=}}. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "th year|" to "年|"
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天之御中主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|qq=}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken Bosatsu|ja|妙見菩薩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken Bosatsu|qq=}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|ja|相馬氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma clan|qq=}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|ja|関ヶ原の戦い|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battle of Sekigahara|qq=}} in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Domain|ja|相馬中村藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nakamura Domain|qq=}}. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|National Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu bunri|qq=}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|ja|戊辰戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Boshin War|qq=}}. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]]

However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]]

The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma City]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Edo-period architecture]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sōma, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[en:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Sōma City]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}}

{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] [[Category:History of Sōma, Fukushima]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}}

However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Sōma Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Sōma Shrine
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg|270px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|47|49.65|N|140|54|48.45|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} | deity = [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|ja|寛永20年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1643|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬市の歴史]] [[Category:相馬神社]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}}

The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}} == Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=}}. After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine. Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]]. The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:相馬市の建築物]] [[Category:相馬の Municipal History]] [[Category:Sōma Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}}

The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment. However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine] == Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar. == images == File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog] ==References== {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Myōken Faith}} {{translated page|ja|相馬中村神社|version=102625679|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Sōma clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties (buildings)]] }} [[sn:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]] [[ja:相馬中村神社]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Soma City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sōma]] [[Category:Sōma Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11582471]] Wikidata name: Sōma Nakamura Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|qid=Q696456}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]
