The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582471]] Wikidata name: Sōma Nakamura Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|qq=|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|qq=|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|qq=|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|qq=|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|qq=|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|qq=|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}

== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.

The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].

'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

<!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--><!--Sōma Nakamura Shrine--> [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Soma City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sōma]] [[Category:Sōma Shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Fukushima prefecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sōma Nakamura Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q11582471}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582471]] Wikidata name: Sōma Nakamura Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582471]] Wikidata name: Sōma Nakamura Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11582471]] Wikidata name: Sōma Nakamura Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}

'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Ame-no-Minakanushi|Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (identified with [[:en:Myōken|Myōken Bosatsu]]). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the [[:en:Sōma clan|Sōma clan]], established a Myōken Shrine in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the [[:en:Battle of Sekigahara|Battle of Sekigahara]] in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the [[:en:Sōma Nakamura Domain|Sōma Nakamura Domain]]. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1643|1643]] by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].
During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the [[:en:Boshin War|Boshin War]].
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}

'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.

The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}.
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>

'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara}} in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Domain|en|Sōma Nakamura Domain|WD=Q844079|lt=Sōma Nakamura Domain}}. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}.
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}.
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sōma Nakamura Shrine]][[ja:相馬中村神社]]
{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Sōma Moritane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara}} in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Domain|en|Sōma Nakamura Domain|WD=Q844079|lt=Sōma Nakamura Domain}}. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}.
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War}}.
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma-Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

{{infobox religious building | name = Sōma Nakamura Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Somanakamura shrine.jpg | location = 140 Nakamura, Kitamachi, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|37.797125|N|140.91345833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} | Shrine Rank = | founded = 1611 (Keichō 16) | main hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Other name = Myōken Nakamura Shrine | festival = April 18–19 | rituals = [[Sōma Nomaoi]] (July 23–25) | map = {{location map|Japan Fukushima Prefecture|label=Sōma Nakamura Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture= {{ill|Sōma|WD=Q696456|1=Sōma, Fukushima}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }}
'''Sōma Nakamura Shrine''' (相馬中村神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Nakamura, Sōma City, Fukushima Prefecture. It is also known as '''Myōken Nakamura Shrine''' (妙見中村神社) or simply Nakamura Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|en|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Minakanushi|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} (identified with {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}). The shrine serves as the starting point for the {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} festival.<ref name="minyu-net20211115">{{Cite web|url=https://www.minyu-net.com/news/news/FM20211115-667668.php |title=Sōma Nakamura Shrine to Receive New Grand Torii After Earthquake Damage |publisher=Fukushima Minyu Newspaper |accessdate=2021-11-15}}</ref>
== Overview == The origins of Sōma Nakamura Shrine trace back to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} when {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the founder of the {{ill|Sōma clan|en|Sōma clan|WD=Q2975379|lt=Sōma clan|1=Sōma clan}}, established a Myōken Shrine in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}} during the Jōhei era (931–937). As the Sōma clan moved to the Sōma district, a new shrine was established. By the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}} in the late 16th century, {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} became the clan's northern stronghold, governed by lords such as {{ill|Sōma Moritane|ja|相馬盛胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Moritane|1=Q11582520|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582520}} and {{ill|Sōma Takamine|ja|相馬隆胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takamine|1=Sōma Takatane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582545}}.
After the {{ill|Battle of Sekigahara|en|Battle of Sekigahara|WD=Q234188|lt=Battle of Sekigahara|1=Battle of Sekigahara}} in 1600, the Sōma clan was briefly dispossessed of their holdings but regained control in 1611, establishing the {{ill|Sōma Nakamura Domain|en|Sōma Nakamura Domain|WD=Q844079|lt=Sōma Nakamura Domain|1=Sōma Nakamura Domain}}. The shrine was built by {{ill|Sōma Takatane|ja|相馬利胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Takatane|1=Sōma Toshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514593}}, the first lord of the Nakamura Domain, near the southwest of Nakamura Castle in 1611. The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643|1=1643}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}.
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
== images == <gallery> File:Soma otemon.JPG|{{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城}} Otemon Gate (2013) File:Nakamura shrine 003.JPG|Entrance to Nakamura Shrine, featuring a horse statue associated with {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}} (2013) </gallery> == See Also == * {{ill|Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja|中村城 (陸奥国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Castle (Mutsu Province)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 相馬中村城|1=Sōma Nakamura Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11582470}} * {{ill|Sōma Nomaoi|ja|相馬野馬追|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Nomaoi|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://soumanakamurajinja.com/ Sōma Nakamura Shrine Official Website] * [https://ameblo.jp/umatoayumu-soma/ Uma to Ayumu SOMA Blog]

The current main shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1643|en|1643|WD=Q6917|lt=1643|1=1643}} by {{ill|Sōma Yoshitane|ja|相馬義胤 (相馬中村藩主)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Yoshitane|1=Sōma Yoshitane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8514636}}, the second domain lord, and has been designated as a {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}.
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} led to the removal of Myōken Bosatsu, and the shrine was renamed Sōma Nakamura Shrine.
Another shrine, {{ill|Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|lt=Sōma Shrine|ja|相馬神社 (相馬市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma Shrine (Sōma City)|qq=}}, was established in 1880 on the ruins of Nakamura Castle's main keep after the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|WD=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}}.
The shrine has been under the care of the Tashiro family, who have served as hereditary priests since its establishment.
The shrine formerly maintained stables for horses, including retired racehorses such as {{ill|Basikeen|ja|バシケーン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Basikeen|1=Bashi Ken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11327620}} (winner of the 2010 Nakayama Grand Jump) and {{ill|Million Disc|ja|ミリオンディスク|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Million Disc|1=Million Disk|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225165}} (winner of the 2009 Capella Stakes). However, the stables were dismantled around 2019.<ref>[https://news.netkeiba.com/?pid=news_view&no=72447 Basikeen Finds a New Home at Sōma Nakamura Shrine]</ref>
== Cultural Properties == '''Important Cultural Properties''' * Main Shrine, Worship Hall, and Offering Hall (1 building) ''' Includes a "kuden" (宮殿) altar.
